description: Hunt technique patterns for scope-hunt hypothesis-driven investigations. Five adversary behavior categories. Each pattern is hypothesis-anchored: starts from 'did this happen?' rather than a confirmed event. Referenced by <hunt_technique_patterns> in scope-hunt.md. Follows config/escalation-catalogue.json convention: versioned root, category-grouped arrays of objects.

credential_abuse.hypothesis_template: An adversary created a new IAM access key for a target user to establish durable programmatic credentials outside normal provisioning.

new_key_creation.name: New access key creation events
new_key_creation.spl:
index=cloudtrail eventName=CreateAccessKey earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| rename userIdentity.userName AS actor, userIdentity.arn AS actor_arn, requestParameters.userName AS target_user
| table _time eventName actor actor_arn target_user sourceIPAddress userAgent recipientAccountId errorCode
| sort _time

key_usage_after_creation.name: Key usage within 2h of creation
key_usage_after_creation.spl:
index=cloudtrail (userIdentity.userName="[target_user]") earliest="[creation_time]" latest="[2h_after_creation]"
| table _time eventName eventSource userIdentity.arn userIdentity.accessKeyId sourceIPAddress userAgent errorCode
| sort _time

prior_key_exists.name: Check if target user already had an active key before the event
prior_key_exists.spl:
index=cloudtrail eventName=CreateAccessKey requestParameters.userName="[target_user]" earliest="[30d_before_event]" latest="[event_time]"
| table _time eventName userIdentity.userName requestParameters.userName sourceIPAddress errorCode
| sort _time

credential_abuse.confirm_criteria: ≥1 CreateAccessKey event for a user outside a known provisioning window, followed by API calls using the new key from a different source IP than the creating actor.
credential_abuse.refute_criteria: CreateAccessKey event matches a known service account provisioning pattern in context.json, or the key was immediately deleted (DeleteAccessKey within 5 min).

credential_abuse.hypothesis_template: An adversary chained AssumeRole calls across multiple roles or accounts to elevate privileges or obscure the originating identity.

role_chain_sequence.name: AssumeRole chain — identify sequential assumptions
role_chain_sequence.spl:
index=cloudtrail eventName=AssumeRole earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| eval assumed_by=coalesce('userIdentity.arn', 'userIdentity.userName')
| stats count, values(requestParameters.roleArn) AS roles_assumed, dc(requestParameters.roleArn) AS unique_roles by assumed_by sourceIPAddress
| where unique_roles > 1
| sort -unique_roles

cross_account_assumption.name: Cross-account AssumeRole — accountId mismatch
cross_account_assumption.spl:
index=cloudtrail eventName=AssumeRole earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| eval is_cross_account=if('userIdentity.accountId'!='recipientAccountId', "YES", "NO")
| where is_cross_account="YES"
| table _time eventName userIdentity.arn requestParameters.roleArn userIdentity.accountId recipientAccountId sourceIPAddress errorCode
| sort _time

baseline_assumption_pattern.name: 7-day baseline for this role — compare to alerting pattern
baseline_assumption_pattern.spl:
index=cloudtrail eventName=AssumeRole requestParameters.roleArn="[target_role_arn]" earliest="[7d_before_event]" latest="[event_time]"
| stats count by userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress
| sort -count

credential_abuse.confirm_criteria: ≥2 sequential AssumeRole calls where the session ARN from call N is the assuming principal in call N+1, or cross-account assumption where userIdentity.accountId != recipientAccountId with no baseline match.
credential_abuse.refute_criteria: All AssumeRole callers and target roles match documented automation or CI/CD service accounts in context.json with consistent source IPs.

credential_abuse.hypothesis_template: An adversary replayed a stolen temporary credential token to access AWS services from an unexpected source.

same_token_different_ip.name: Same access key ID used from multiple source IPs
same_token_different_ip.spl:
index=cloudtrail earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| stats dc(sourceIPAddress) AS ip_count, values(sourceIPAddress) AS source_ips, values(eventName) AS events by userIdentity.accessKeyId
| where ip_count > 1 AND isnotnull(userIdentity.accessKeyId)
| sort -ip_count

session_token_source_deviation.name: Assumed role session used from IP inconsistent with assumption origin
session_token_source_deviation.spl:
index=cloudtrail eventName=AssumeRole earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| eval session_arn=responseElements.assumedRoleUser.arn
| eval assumption_ip=sourceIPAddress
| join session_arn [search index=cloudtrail "userIdentity.arn"="[assumed_session_arn]" earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]" | eval usage_ip=sourceIPAddress | table session_arn usage_ip]
| where assumption_ip!=usage_ip
| table _time eventName session_arn assumption_ip usage_ip
| sort _time

credential_abuse.confirm_criteria: Same access key ID or assumed-role session ARN used from ≥2 distinct source IPs, where one IP does not match the known principal location in context.json.
credential_abuse.refute_criteria: All source IPs for the token are consistent with known NAT gateways, VPN egress IPs, or CI/CD runner IPs documented in context.json.

data_exfiltration.hypothesis_template: An adversary performed a bulk read of S3 objects from a sensitive bucket to stage data for exfiltration.

s3_list_then_get.name: ListBuckets/ListObjects followed by GetObject — reconnaissance to exfil sequence
s3_list_then_get.spl:
index=cloudtrail eventSource=s3.amazonaws.com (eventName=ListBuckets OR eventName=ListObjects OR eventName=GetObject) (userIdentity.arn="[user_arn]" OR userIdentity.userName="[user_name]") earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| table _time eventName requestParameters.bucketName requestParameters.key userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress errorCode
| sort _time

s3_getobject_volume.name: GetObject volume spike — compare to 7d baseline
s3_getobject_volume.spl:
index=cloudtrail eventName=GetObject eventSource=s3.amazonaws.com requestParameters.bucketName="[bucket_name]" earliest="[7d_before_event]" latest="[event_time]"
| bucket _time span=1h
| stats count by _time userIdentity.arn
| sort _time

data_exfiltration.confirm_criteria: GetObject event count from a single principal in a 1-hour window exceeds 2x the 7-day hourly baseline, or GetObject events occur from a source IP with no prior access to the bucket.
data_exfiltration.refute_criteria: GetObject volume matches scheduled backup job or known ETL pipeline source IP documented in context.json.

data_exfiltration.hypothesis_template: An adversary bulk-retrieved secrets from Secrets Manager or SSM Parameter Store to harvest credentials for downstream services.

secrets_bulk_read.name: Bulk GetSecretValue — multiple distinct secrets in short window
secrets_bulk_read.spl:
index=cloudtrail eventName=GetSecretValue earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| stats count, dc(requestParameters.secretId) AS unique_secrets, values(requestParameters.secretId) AS secrets_accessed by userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress
| where unique_secrets > 3
| sort -unique_secrets

ssm_parameter_harvest.name: SSM GetParameter calls — credential parameter paths
ssm_parameter_harvest.spl:
index=cloudtrail (eventName=GetParameter OR eventName=GetParameters) earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| where like(requestParameters.name, "%password%") OR like(requestParameters.name, "%secret%") OR like(requestParameters.name, "%key%") OR like(requestParameters.name, "%token%")
| table _time eventName requestParameters.name userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress errorCode
| sort _time

secrets_list_before_read.name: ListSecrets reconnaissance before GetSecretValue
secrets_list_before_read.spl:
index=cloudtrail eventSource=secretsmanager.amazonaws.com (eventName=ListSecrets OR eventName=GetSecretValue) earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| table _time eventName requestParameters.secretId userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress errorCode
| sort _time

data_exfiltration.confirm_criteria: ≥1 GetSecretValue or GetParameter call from a principal that is not a known application service account, or bulk reads (≥4 distinct secrets in ≤10 min) regardless of principal.
data_exfiltration.refute_criteria: All GetSecretValue calls match documented service account ARNs in context.json with consistent source IPs and no anomalous timing.

data_exfiltration.hypothesis_template: An adversary created and shared an RDS or EBS snapshot with an external AWS account to exfiltrate data outside the organization.

snapshot_share_external.name: ModifySnapshotAttribute — add external account to restore list
snapshot_share_external.spl:
index=cloudtrail (eventName=ModifySnapshotAttribute OR eventName=ModifyDBSnapshotAttribute) earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| eval shared_with=coalesce('requestParameters.createVolumePermission.add.items{}.userId', 'requestParameters.attributeValues.restore.items{}.id')
| where isnotnull(shared_with)
| table _time eventName requestParameters.snapshotId requestParameters.dbSnapshotIdentifier shared_with userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress errorCode
| sort _time

snapshot_create_then_share.name: CreateSnapshot followed by ModifySnapshotAttribute in short window
snapshot_create_then_share.spl:
index=cloudtrail (eventName=CreateSnapshot OR eventName=CreateDBSnapshot OR eventName=ModifySnapshotAttribute OR eventName=ModifyDBSnapshotAttribute) earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| table _time eventName requestParameters.snapshotId requestParameters.dbSnapshotIdentifier requestParameters.volumeId userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress errorCode
| sort _time

data_exfiltration.confirm_criteria: ModifySnapshotAttribute or ModifyDBSnapshotAttribute event adds an account ID not present in config/accounts.json (external account) to the restore/shared list, within 24h of snapshot creation.
data_exfiltration.refute_criteria: The account ID added to the snapshot share list is present in config/accounts.json (internal account), or the snapshot was immediately deleted before external access was possible.

persistence.hypothesis_template: An adversary created or modified an IAM role trust policy to allow an external principal or attacker-controlled account to assume the role as a persistent backdoor.

trust_policy_modification.name: UpdateAssumeRolePolicy — trust policy changes
trust_policy_modification.spl:
index=cloudtrail eventName=UpdateAssumeRolePolicy earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| table _time eventName requestParameters.roleName requestParameters.policyDocument userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress errorCode
| sort _time

new_role_external_trust.name: CreateRole with external account in trust policy
new_role_external_trust.spl:
index=cloudtrail eventName=CreateRole earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| where like(requestParameters.assumeRolePolicyDocument, "%sts:AssumeRole%")
| table _time eventName requestParameters.roleName requestParameters.assumeRolePolicyDocument userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress errorCode
| sort _time

backdoor_role_assumption.name: AssumeRole calls for the newly created/modified role
backdoor_role_assumption.spl:
index=cloudtrail eventName=AssumeRole requestParameters.roleArn="[new_or_modified_role_arn]" earliest="[modification_time]" latest="[7d_after_modification]"
| table _time eventName userIdentity.arn requestParameters.roleArn requestParameters.roleSessionName sourceIPAddress errorCode
| sort _time

persistence.confirm_criteria: CreateRole or UpdateAssumeRolePolicy event where the trust policy document contains an account ID not in config/accounts.json, followed by ≥1 AssumeRole call from that external account.
persistence.refute_criteria: All principals in the trust policy document are in config/accounts.json (internal accounts), or the role was created by a documented IaC pipeline (Terraform, CloudFormation) service account.

persistence.hypothesis_template: An adversary created a new IAM user with console or programmatic access to maintain persistent access under a separate identity.

new_user_with_access.name: CreateUser followed by CreateLoginProfile or CreateAccessKey
new_user_with_access.spl:
index=cloudtrail eventSource=iam.amazonaws.com (eventName=CreateUser OR eventName=CreateLoginProfile OR eventName=CreateAccessKey OR eventName=AttachUserPolicy) earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| table _time eventName requestParameters.userName requestParameters.policyArn userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress errorCode
| sort _time

ghost_user_activity.name: API calls by newly created user after provisioning
ghost_user_activity.spl:
index=cloudtrail userIdentity.userName="[new_user_name]" earliest="[creation_time]" latest="[7d_after_creation]"
| table _time eventName eventSource userIdentity.arn userIdentity.accessKeyId sourceIPAddress userAgent errorCode
| sort _time

persistence.confirm_criteria: CreateUser event not matching a known provisioning workflow, followed by CreateLoginProfile or CreateAccessKey for that user within 60 min, then API activity from the new user.
persistence.refute_criteria: CreateUser event matches a documented automated provisioning pipeline service account in context.json with expected timing and policy attachment.

persistence.hypothesis_template: An adversary modified or created a Lambda function to implant malicious code that executes on a schedule or trigger.

lambda_code_change.name: Lambda CreateFunction or UpdateFunctionCode events
lambda_code_change.spl:
index=cloudtrail (eventName=CreateFunction OR eventName=UpdateFunctionCode) earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| table _time eventName requestParameters.functionName requestParameters.code.s3Bucket requestParameters.code.imageUri userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress errorCode
| sort _time

scheduled_trigger_added.name: EventBridge rule + target for Lambda scheduling
scheduled_trigger_added.spl:
index=cloudtrail (eventName=PutRule OR eventName=PutTargets) earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| table _time eventName requestParameters.name requestParameters.scheduleExpression requestParameters.targets userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress errorCode
| sort _time

persistence.confirm_criteria: UpdateFunctionCode or CreateFunction by a principal that is not a documented CI/CD service account, or new EventBridge rule targeting a Lambda function created in the same time window.
persistence.refute_criteria: All Lambda code changes match documented deployment pipeline service account ARNs in context.json with expected source IPs.

lateral_movement.hypothesis_template: An adversary used AssumeRole to pivot from a compromised identity in one AWS account to a role in a different account, expanding the attack surface.

cross_account_assume.name: AssumeRole where caller account != recipient account
cross_account_assume.spl:
index=cloudtrail eventName=AssumeRole earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| where 'userIdentity.accountId'!='recipientAccountId'
| table _time eventName userIdentity.arn userIdentity.accountId requestParameters.roleArn recipientAccountId requestParameters.roleSessionName sourceIPAddress errorCode
| sort _time

post_pivot_activity.name: Activity by the assumed role session in destination account
post_pivot_activity.spl:
index=cloudtrail "userIdentity.arn"="[assumed_role_session_arn]" earliest="[assumption_time]" latest="[2h_after_assumption]"
| table _time eventName eventSource userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress userAgent errorCode
| sort _time

pivot_baseline.name: 7-day baseline — who normally assumes this cross-account role?
pivot_baseline.spl:
index=cloudtrail eventName=AssumeRole requestParameters.roleArn="[target_role_arn]" earliest="[7d_before_event]" latest="[event_time]"
| stats count by userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress
| sort -count

lateral_movement.confirm_criteria: AssumeRole event where userIdentity.accountId != recipientAccountId, with the assuming principal being a recently compromised or anomalous identity, and post-assumption activity in the destination account.
lateral_movement.refute_criteria: Cross-account assumption matches a documented service integration or CI/CD trust relationship in context.json with expected source IP.

lateral_movement.hypothesis_template: An adversary used AWS Systems Manager to execute commands or start interactive sessions on EC2 instances to move laterally within the environment.

ssm_command_execution.name: SSM SendCommand or StartSession events
ssm_command_execution.spl:
index=cloudtrail (eventName=SendCommand OR eventName=StartSession OR eventName=ExecuteCommand) earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| table _time eventName requestParameters.instanceIds requestParameters.documentName requestParameters.target userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress errorCode
| sort _time

ssm_session_actor_baseline.name: 7-day baseline for SSM usage by this actor
ssm_session_actor_baseline.spl:
index=cloudtrail (eventName=SendCommand OR eventName=StartSession) userIdentity.arn="[actor_arn]" earliest="[7d_before_event]" latest="[event_time]"
| stats count by requestParameters.instanceIds
| sort -count

lateral_movement.confirm_criteria: SendCommand or StartSession by a principal with no prior SSM usage in context.json, or SSM commands targeting instances in a different VPC or account than the actor's normal scope.
lateral_movement.refute_criteria: SSM session matches a documented admin maintenance window or runbook automation service account in context.json.

defense_evasion.hypothesis_template: An adversary stopped, deleted, or modified CloudTrail logging to create a visibility gap before or during malicious activity.

cloudtrail_modification.name: CloudTrail StopLogging, DeleteTrail, UpdateTrail, PutEventSelectors
cloudtrail_modification.spl:
index=cloudtrail (eventName=StopLogging OR eventName=DeleteTrail OR eventName=UpdateTrail OR eventName=PutEventSelectors) earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| table _time eventName requestParameters.name requestParameters.trailName requestParameters.eventSelectors userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress userAgent errorCode
| sort _time

activity_during_gap.name: Actor activity during the suppression window
activity_during_gap.spl:
index=cloudtrail (userIdentity.arn="[actor_arn]" OR userIdentity.userName="[actor_name]") earliest="[modification_time]" latest="[1h_after_modification]"
| table _time eventName eventSource userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress userAgent errorCode
| sort _time

logging_restored.name: Check if StartLogging was called — rapid restoration may indicate testing
logging_restored.spl:
index=cloudtrail eventName=StartLogging (requestParameters.name="[trail_name]" OR requestParameters.trailName="[trail_name]") earliest="[modification_time]" latest="[4h_after_modification]"
| table _time eventName userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress
| sort _time

defense_evasion.confirm_criteria: StopLogging or DeleteTrail event not matching a documented maintenance change window in context.json, especially when followed by unusual actor activity during the logging gap.
defense_evasion.refute_criteria: StopLogging event is immediately followed by StartLogging within 5 min (testing pattern), or the change matches a documented change management ticket or IaC pipeline activity.

defense_evasion.hypothesis_template: An adversary disabled or disassociated GuardDuty to prevent threat detection alerts during an active attack.

guardduty_disable.name: GuardDuty detector deletion or disassociation
guardduty_disable.spl:
index=cloudtrail eventSource=guardduty.amazonaws.com (eventName=DeleteDetector OR eventName=DisassociateFromMasterAccount OR eventName=DisassociateMembers OR eventName=UpdateDetector) earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| table _time eventName requestParameters.detectorId requestParameters.enable userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress errorCode
| sort _time

config_recorder_disable.name: AWS Config StopConfigurationRecorder or DeleteDeliveryChannel (co-occurring evasion)
config_recorder_disable.spl:
index=cloudtrail (eventName=StopConfigurationRecorder OR eventName=DeleteDeliveryChannel) earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| table _time eventName requestParameters.configurationRecorderName requestParameters.deliveryChannelName userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress errorCode
| sort _time

defense_evasion.confirm_criteria: DeleteDetector or UpdateDetector with enable=false event from a non-security-automation principal, or disassociation of a member account from GuardDuty master account.
defense_evasion.refute_criteria: GuardDuty change matches a documented security tool migration or region consolidation project documented in context.json, followed by CreateDetector in the same region within 60 min.

defense_evasion.hypothesis_template: An adversary stopped AWS Config recording or deleted delivery channels to prevent configuration change tracking.

config_stop.name: Config recorder stopped or delivery channel deleted
config_stop.spl:
index=cloudtrail eventSource=config.amazonaws.com (eventName=StopConfigurationRecorder OR eventName=DeleteDeliveryChannel OR eventName=DeleteConfigurationRecorder) earliest="[time_range_earliest]" latest="[time_range_latest]"
| table _time eventName requestParameters.configurationRecorderName requestParameters.deliveryChannelName userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress errorCode
| sort _time

config_actor_full_timeline.name: Full actor timeline 2h before and after Config change
config_actor_full_timeline.spl:
index=cloudtrail (userIdentity.arn="[actor_arn]" OR userIdentity.userName="[actor_name]") earliest="[2h_before_event]" latest="[2h_after_event]"
| table _time eventName eventSource userIdentity.arn sourceIPAddress userAgent errorCode
| sort _time

defense_evasion.confirm_criteria: StopConfigurationRecorder or DeleteDeliveryChannel by a principal that is not a documented IaC or security automation service account, especially when co-occurring with CloudTrail modification or GuardDuty disable.
defense_evasion.refute_criteria: Config recorder stop matches a documented account decommission or region migration, followed by StartConfigurationRecorder within the same maintenance window.
